And so, you know, remember that you have standards for everything, not just for money and success. That's one aspect of the equation. And if you have a very high money standard, but really a very low standard of how you treat other people, then guess what? You know, you're going to be a wealthy asshole. Right. No one can give it to you or take it away. It's what you give yourself. And there is no right or wrong standard for money. The answer is what serves you. What is it that's going to really make you feel good every single day when you get out of bed? You know, you know, are you congruent or are you walking around with a big dream and a vision for your future, but you just can't really get yourself to do the necessary things day in and day out to turn that into a reality. Just to get the first step here, and we're going to go through the steps this week of how you raise your standard. The first step is getting honest with yourself. You got to get honest and just say, you know, where is my standard? What is it that I demand of myself when it comes to making money? If I make less than X dollars, I'll work, you know, seven days a week. I know what my number is that I can tell I'm making that amount. I'm like on a full blown tear because that's my standard. I don't feel right unless I hit it. And I got to tell you something you know, well, the first one to admit that money is not the fuel end all. If you're standing for money is like a hundred thousand a year, you're not getting anywhere in this world. I'm just letting you know that you need to make more. You're gonna have to ramp that up.
